creator: xor@davydkovo.net
text:
After upgrading of system from FreeBSD 8 Current to FreeBSD 9.0 PRERELEASE, the port 'ucarp' has ceased to be started.

How-To-Repeat: .ucarp working..
..Upgrading from FreeBSD 8 to 9...

# /usr/local/etc/rc.d/ucarp start

# ps -wwaux | grep ucarp
xor       52224   0.0  0.0  16424   1132   6  S+   10:12PM    0:00.00 grep ucarp

# grep ucarp /var/log/messages
Jan 12 22:15:12 ucarp[52823]: [ERROR] Invalid media / hardware address for [vlan8]
Jan 12 22:15:12 ucarp[52823]: [ERROR] Unable to find MAC address of [vlan8]

# ifconfig vlan8
vlan8: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=3<RXCSUM,TXCSUM>
        ether 00:04:23:d1:26:4c
        inet6 fe80::204:23ff:fed1:264c%vlan8 prefixlen 64 scopeid 0x14
        inet 192.168.255.254 netmask 0xfffffff8 broadcast 192.168.255.255
        nd6 options=23<PERFORMNUD,ACCEPT_RTADV,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
        media: Ethernet autoselect
        status: active
        vlan: 8 parent interface: lagg0

# grep ucarp /etc/rc.conf
ucarp_enable="YES"
ucarp_addr="192.168.255.253"
ucarp_if="vlan8"
ucarp_src="192.168.255.254"
ucarp_pass="somepass"
ucarp_upscript="/usr/local/etc/ucarp-up"
ucarp_downscript="/usr/local/etc/ucarp-down"
